1	/**
2	 * Integration test: lesson duplication orchestrator.
3	 *
4	 * Acceptance criteria:
5	 *  1. Orchestrator runs at most 4 strategy calls in parallel
6	 *  2. One exercise failing does not abort the run — remaining exercises complete
7	 *  3. Failures appear in the DB record as the run progresses (live streaming, asserted by polling)
8	 *  4. Final status is succeeded only when failures array is empty, else needs_review
9	 *  5. 5-exercise lesson with one forced failure → needs_review with 4 succeeded + 1 failure entry
10	 */
11	import { afterAll, beforeAll, beforeEach, describe, expect, it, vi } from 'vitest'
12	import { getPayload, type Payload } from 'payload'
13	import config from '@payload-config'
14	
15	import { getDefaultTenantSlug } from '@/server/repos/tenant/get-default-tenant'
16	import { runDuplicationOrchestrator } from '@/server/services/lesson-duplication/orchestrator'
17

151	async function ensureDefaultTenant(payload: Payload): Promise<string> {
152	  const slug = getDefaultTenantSlug()
153	  const existing = await payload.find({
154	    collection: 'tenants',
155	    where: { slug: { equals: slug } },
156	    limit: 1,
157	    overrideAccess: true,
158	  })
159	  if (existing.docs[0]) return existing.docs[0].id
160	  const created = await payload.create({
161	    collection: 'tenants',
162	    data: { name: slug, slug, status: 'active' },
163	    overrideAccess: true,
164	  })
165	  return created.id
166	}
167	
168	describe('Lesson duplication orchestrator — integration', () => {
169	  let payload: Payload
170	  let categoryId: string
171	  let courseId: string
172	  let chapterId: string
173	  let tenantId: string
174	  let sourceLessonId: string
175	  const cleanupLessonIds: string[] = []
176	  const cleanupExerciseIds: string[] = []
177	  const cleanupDuplicationIds: string[] = []
178	
179	  beforeEach(() => {
180	<<<<<<< HEAD

182	=======
183	    _vgCallCount = 0
184	>>>>>>> origin/dev
185	  })
186	
187	  beforeAll(async () => {
188	    payload = await getPayload({ config })
189	    tenantId = await ensureDefaultTenant(payload)
190	    const ts = Date.now()
191	
192	    const category = await payload.create({
193	      collection: 'categories',
194	      data: { title: `OrchCat ${ts}`, slug: `orch-cat-${ts}`, locale: 'he' },
195	    })
196	    categoryId = category.id
197	
198	    const course = await payload.create({
199	      collection: 'courses',
200	      data: {
201	        courseLabel: `ORCH-${ts}`,
202	        title: `Orch Course ${ts}`,
203	        locale: 'he',
204	        categories: [categoryId],
205	        order: 0,
206	        status: 'published',
207	        isActive: true,
208	        tenant: tenantId,
209	        pageAccessType: 'free',
210	        accessType: 'free',
211	        contentStatus: 'none',
212	        contentStatusVisible: true,
213	      },
214	      draft: false,
215	    })
216	    courseId = course.id
217	
218	    const chapter = await payload.create({
219	      collection: 'chapters',
220	      data: {
221	        title: `Orch Chapter ${ts}`,
222	        chapterLabel: `OCH-${ts}`,
223	        course: courseId,
224	        order: 0,
225	        status: 'published',
226	        isActive: true,
227	        tenant: tenantId,
228	        locale: 'he',
229	      },
230	    })
231	    chapterId = chapter.id
232	
233	    const lesson = await payload.create({
234	      collection: 'lessons',
235	      data: {
236	        title: `Orch Source Lesson ${ts}`,
237	        chapter: chapterId,
238	        type: 'practice',
239	        order: 1,
240	        status: 'published',
241	        isActive: true,
242	        tenant: tenantId,
243	        locale: 'he',
244	        accessType: 'inherit',
245	        contentStatus: 'none',
246	        contentStatusVisible: true,
247	      },
248	      draft: false,
249	    })
250	    sourceLessonId = lesson.id
251	    cleanupLessonIds.push(sourceLessonId)
252	
253	    // Create exactly 5 exercises on the source lesson
254	    for (let i = 0; i < 5; i++) {
255	      const ex = await payload.create({
256	        collection: 'exercises',
257	        data: { title: `Orch Exercise ${i} ${ts}`, lesson: sourceLessonId },
258	        draft: true,
259	      })
260	      cleanupExerciseIds.push(ex.id)
261	    }
262	  }, 120000)
263	
264	  afterAll(async () => {
265	    for (const id of cleanupDuplicationIds) {
266	      try {
267	        await payload.delete({ collection: 'lesson-duplications', id, overrideAccess: true })
268	      } catch {
269	        /* ignore */
270	      }
271	    }
272	    for (const id of cleanupExerciseIds) {
273	      try {
274	        await payload.delete({ collection: 'exercises', id, overrideAccess: true })
275	      } catch {
276	        /* ignore */
277	      }
278	    }
279	    for (const id of cleanupLessonIds) {
280	      try {
281	        await payload.delete({ collection: 'lessons', id, overrideAccess: true })
282	      } catch {
283	        /* ignore */
284	      }
285	    }
286	    try {
287	      await payload.delete({ collection: 'chapters', id: chapterId, overrideAccess: true })
288	    } catch {
289	      /* ignore */
290	    }
291	    try {
292	      await payload.delete({ collection: 'courses', id: courseId, overrideAccess: true })
293	    } catch {
294	      /* ignore */
295	    }
296	    try {
297	      await payload.delete({ collection: 'categories', id: categoryId, overrideAccess: true })
298	    } catch {
299	      /* ignore */
300	    }
301	    await payload.db?.destroy?.()
302	  })
303	
304	  /**
305	   * Poll the duplication record until status is no longer 'running' or 'pending'.
306	   * Returns the final record state.
307	   */
308	  async function pollUntilDone(recordId: string): Promise<{
309	    status: string
310	    // eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any
311	    failures: any[]
312	
313	    outputLesson?: string | null
314	    // eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any
315	    outputExercises?: any[]
316	  }> {
317	    let record = await payload.findByID({
318	      collection: 'lesson-duplications',
319	      id: recordId,
320	      depth: 0,
321	      overrideAccess: true,
322	    })
323	    const deadline = Date.now() + 30000
324	    while ((record.status === 'running' || record.status === 'pending') && Date.now() < deadline) {
325	      await new Promise((r) => setTimeout(r, 500))
326	      record = await payload.findByID({
327	        collection: 'lesson-duplications',
328	        id: recordId,
329	        depth: 0,
330	        overrideAccess: true,
331	      })
332	    }
333	    // eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any
334	    return record as any
335	  }
336	
337	  it('5-exercise lesson with one forced failure ends in needs_review with failures recorded', async () => {
338	    // Create pending duplication record
339	    const record = await payload.create({
340	      collection: 'lesson-duplications',
341	      data: { sourceLesson: sourceLessonId, level: 'medium', status: 'pending' },
342	      overrideAccess: true,
343	    })
344	    cleanupDuplicationIds.push(record.id)
345	
346	    expect(record.status).toBe('pending')
347	
348	    // Run orchestrator (mocked generateVariation throws on 5th call)
349	    try {
350	      await runDuplicationOrchestrator(record.id, payload)
351	    } catch {
352	      // Orchestrator may throw if the mock is not properly applied;
353	      // we still verify the DB record state below
354	    }
355	
356	    // Poll until done
357	    const finalRecord = await pollUntilDone(record.id)
358	
359	    // Final status must be needs_review (not succeeded, not failed)
360	    expect(finalRecord.status).toBe('needs_review')
361	
362	    // At least one failure entry expected (5th exercise throws in generateVariation)
363	    expect(finalRecord.failures).toBeDefined()
364	    expect(finalRecord.failures.length).toBeGreaterThanOrEqual(1)
365	    expect(finalRecord.failures[0].code).toBe('GENERATION_FAILED')
366	    expect(finalRecord.failures[0].suggestedAction).toBe('skip')
367	
368	    // After orchestrator runs, outputLesson should be created
369	    // Note: outputExercises remain empty because the mock bypasses
370	    // createOutputExercise. Exercise creation is verified in
371	    // lesson-duplication-review-resolve.int.spec.ts instead.
372	    expect(finalRecord.outputLesson).toBeTruthy()
373	  }, 300000)
374	
375	  it('orchestrator does not abort when one exercise fails — remaining exercises are processed', async () => {
376	    // Create fresh pending record
377	    const record = await payload.create({
378	      collection: 'lesson-duplications',
379	      data: { sourceLesson: sourceLessonId, level: 'medium', status: 'pending' },
380	      overrideAccess: true,
381	    })
382	    cleanupDuplicationIds.push(record.id)
383	
384	    // Run orchestrator; it may throw if the mock isn't applied (that's ok for this test)
385	    try {
386	      await runDuplicationOrchestrator(record.id, payload)
387	    } catch {
388	      // ignore
389	    }
390	
391	    const finalRecord = await pollUntilDone(record.id)
392	
393	    // The orchestrator should reach needs_review (not 'failed'), meaning it processed
394	    // exercises and collected failures rather than crashing completely.
395	    // If the orchestrator crashed early, status would be 'failed' with 0 failures.
396	    expect(finalRecord.status).toBe('needs_review')
397	
398	    // At least one failure means at least one exercise failed and the orchestrator
399	    // continued processing other exercises (did not abort on first failure).
400	    expect(finalRecord.failures).toBeDefined()
401	    expect(finalRecord.failures.length).toBeGreaterThanOrEqual(1)
402	    expect(finalRecord.failures[0].code).toBe('GENERATION_FAILED')
403	
404	    // outputLesson should be created even when some exercises fail
405	    expect(finalRecord.outputLesson).toBeTruthy()
406	  }, 300000)
407	})

content.thinking:
Now I understand the conflict. Let me analyze both sides:

**HEAD (PR #1480 — `kody-memorize-20260507`):**
- Uses `generateVariationCallCount` as a counter
- Forces failure on call 5+ (`generateVariationCallCount >= 5`)
- Simple mock implementation, inline

**origin/dev:**
- Uses `_vgCallCount` as a counter  
- Forces failure on call 3 (`_vgCallCount === 3`)
- More detailed mock with explicit typing and Promise return type annotation
- Adds `_payload: unknown` parameter (accepts a second argument)


- Both versions accomplish the same thing: they mock `generateVariation` to fail on specific calls and return mock exercise data, just with different triggering points and parameter signatures.

Now I'm looking at the `beforeEach` hooks to see what gets reset before each test—the HEAD version resets the call counter to 0, while the origin/dev version does the same. Both implementations are essentially equivalent, but I should go with the origin/dev version since it's more recent and represents the updated test setup.

The key difference is the failure threshold: the HEAD version fails on the 5th call, whereas the dev version fails on the 3rd call. Looking at the acceptance criteria, the test expects one forced failure among 5 exercises total, which aligns better with the dev version's earlier failure trigger. The dev version also has better type annotation with the explicit `Promise` return type, so I'm leaning toward taking that approach.

For the beforeEach reset, the dev version is more explicit about the naming convention with the underscore prefix. I'll use the dev version as the primary source and write out the merged file with that approach.

content.text:
Now I understand the conflict. Both sides mock the same function with equivalent logic, but with different call-count thresholds and type annotations. The `origin/dev` version is more recent and has better type annotations. The beforeEach reset conflict is also symmetric.

I'll take `origin/dev` for the mock implementation and beforeEach reset.

// Mocks at the LLM call site (generateVariation), intercepting before any
// network call is made. CONCURRENCY_LIMIT=1 ensures deterministic ordering.
let generateVariationCallCount = 0
const MOCK_BLOCKS = [
  {
    id: 'q-1',
    type: 'question_select',
    variant: 'mcq',
    selectionMode: 'single',
    prompt: { type: 'rich_text', format: 'md-math-v1', value: 'What is 2+2?', mediaIds: [] },
    answer: {
      multiSelect: false,
      options: [
        { id: 'a', content: { type: 'rich_text', format: 'md-math-v1', value: '3', mediaIds: [] } },
        { id: 'b', content: { type: 'rich_text', format: 'md-math-v1', value: '4', mediaIds: [] } },
      ],
      correctOptionIds: ['b'],
    },
    hint: { type: 'rich_text', format: 'md-math-v1', value: 'Think arithmetic', mediaIds: [] },
    solution: { type: 'rich_text', format: 'md-math-v1', value: '2+2=4', mediaIds: [] },
    fullSolution: {
      type: 'rich_text',
      format: 'md-math-v1',
      value: 'Basic addition',
      mediaIds: [],
    },
  },
]

file.content:
/**
 * Integration test: lesson duplication orchestrator.
 *
 * Acceptance criteria:
 *  1. Orchestrator runs at most 4 strategy calls in parallel
 *  2. One exercise failing does not abort the run — remaining exercises complete
 *  3. Failures appear in the DB record as the run progresses (live streaming, asserted by polling)
 *  4. Final status is succeeded only when failures array is empty, else needs_review
 *  5. 5-exercise lesson with one forced failure → needs_review with 4 succeeded + 1 failure entry
 */
import { afterAll, beforeAll, beforeEach, describe, expect, it, vi } from 'vitest'
import { getPayload, type Payload } from 'payload'
import config from '@payload-config'

import { getDefaultTenantSlug } from '@/server/repos/tenant/get-default-tenant'
import { runDuplicationOrchestrator } from '@/server/services/lesson-duplication/orchestrator'

// Mock generateVariation to inject failures without hitting the LLM.
// Mocks at the LLM call site (generateVariation), intercepting before any
// network call is made. CONCURRENCY_LIMIT=1 ensures deterministic ordering.
let generateVariationCallCount = 0
const MOCK_BLOCKS = [
  {
    id: 'q-1',
    type: 'question_select',
    variant: 'mcq',
    selectionMode: 'single',
    prompt: { type: 'rich_text', format: 'md-math-v1', value: 'What is 2+2?', mediaIds: [] },
    answer: {
      multiSelect: false,
      options: [
        { id: 'a', content: { type: 'rich_text', format: 'md-math-v1', value: '3', mediaIds: [] } },
        { id: 'b', content: { type: 'rich_text', format: 'md-math-v1', value: '4', mediaIds: [] } },
      ],
      correctOptionIds: ['b'],
    },
    hint: { type: 'rich_text', format: 'md-math-v1', value: 'Think arithmetic', mediaIds: [] },
    solution: { type: 'rich_text', format: 'md-math-v1', value: '2+2=4', mediaIds: [] },
    fullSolution: {
      type: 'rich_text',
      format: 'md-math-v1',
      value: 'Basic addition',
      mediaIds: [],
    },
  },
]
vi.mock('@/infra/llm/services/lesson-duplication-variation-service', () => ({
  generateVariation: vi.fn().mockImplementation(async (params: { exercise: { id: string } }) => {
    generateVariationCallCount++
